AC #1-3: Dialog shows elements, accepts input, and supports memo
AC #4: Create button state and emits code_created signal
Cancel button closes dialog without creating
AC #1-2: Selecting a code sets it active and tracks recent codes
AC #1-2: Text selection enables quick mark and Q key applies code
AC #3: Quick mark without code does nothing
AC #1-3: U key removes code, enables undo, and Ctrl+Z undoes unmark
Recent codes MRU order, 10-item limit, and all shortcuts registered
Full workflow: Create code via dialog, select, apply
Quick mark persists segment to database and emits signal bridge event
Screen is properly configured after wiring
Multiple segments can be created and navigation loads source
Navigation loads source and database codes are available
All screens created, coding functional, and signal bridges running
N key opens dialog and creating code persists to database
Created code appears in panel and can be applied immediately
Dialog closes after creation, cancel does not create, and duplicate name rejected
Plus button opens dialog and creates code that persists

AC #1-2: Open existing project, verify contexts initialized
AC #1: Open fails for non-existent file and invalid database
AC #3-4: Previously saved sources loaded and recent projects tracked
AC #1-3: Create project with name/location, valid schema, and openable
AC #1-2: Create fails if exists, and new project starts empty
AC #1: Empty and populated sources list
AC #2: Sources show name, type, status, and multiple types
AC #3-4: Sources can be filtered by type and selected as current
AC #4-5: Context preserved when switching and all screens accessible
AC #5: Agent queries project context (open and closed)
AC #5: Agent lists sources and filters by type
AC #2-4: Agent reads source content and gets current source
AC #6: Navigate tool schema, validation, and nonexistent source
AC #4-6: Navigation publishes event and suggest metadata
Complete workflow: Create, open, add sources, query, close
test_segment_management_e2e
AC #2.1-2: Apply multiple codes to same and overlapping selections
AC #3.1-2: Navigate to segment and quick mark state tracking
AC #4.1-2: Codes show segment counts and screen supports active code filtering
AC #6.1-3: Dialog shows preview, code info, and user can enter memo text

Panel emits memo_clicked on selection

AC #4.1: Merge overlapping codes (Focused Coding)

test_thread_safe_repos_e2e
Engine uses SingletonThreadPool and factory returns same conn on same thread
Factory returns different conn on different thread with busy_timeout
Worker and main threads can read each other's writes
MCP worker thread and UI main thread can access repos concurrently
_execute_tool runs safely via asyncio.to_thread for reads and writes
Project close disposes engine and clears connection_factory

AC #3: Screen displays snapshot cards and supports refresh
AC #4: Dialog displays diff content, refs, and correct stats
